title: Nested Data Structures
scenario: Write a Python function that takes a nested dictionary as input and returns a flattened dictionary with the following key structure: '{key1}.{key2}.{key3}': 'value'. The function should recursively traverse the nested dictionary and handle nested lists as well.
constraints: The code should be written in Python.
The function should handle nested lists of arbitrary depth.

evaluationCriteria.description: The output meets all constraints specified in the prompt.
The function correctly flattens the nested dictionary.
The code is well-structured, readable, and follows standard Python conventions.
